description: Thoughts and things I notice. Not long enough for a post, too interesting to keep to myself. I write them here with the date.
notesIntro: Scattered thoughts, opinions on tools, things I notice. No claim to be right—just things that seemed worth writing down.

logs.content: Voice agents are ready for customer service—calls, support, outbound—but almost nobody has a clear way to measure their value yet. Classic ROI and handle time don't map cleanly; you need to track resolution, escalation, and satisfaction in a way that fits the new flow. We're going to need a new framework before adoption really scales.

n8n keeps showing up as the default for automation: workflows, webhooks, glue between APIs. It's not the prettiest or the most "enterprise" tool, but it's the one I end up using when I need something done fast. The node-based UI and self-host option make it easy to iterate without locking into a vendor.

Local inference is changing the whole conversation. When you can run a capable model on your own hardware, questions about privacy, cost, and latency get answered differently. No more "send it to the cloud" by default—you choose where it runs. That shift is already visible in dev tools and will reach product soon.
